headline: Restaurateur Finds $12,000 in Antique Cabinet, Tracks Down Owner
meta_description: A Myrtle Beach restaurant owner discovered $12,000 in an old cabinet and went on live TV to reunite the money with its rightful owner.
summary_short: When Sak Yiengjuntuek found $12,000 hidden in a sunglasses case inside an antique cabinet at his restaurant, he refused to keep it. His search for the previous owner led…
body:
When Sak Yiengjuntuek opened an old sunglasses case inside a forgotten tool chest at his newly acquired restaurant, he discovered something unexpected: a stack of $100 bills totaling $12,000.

The Myrtle Beach restaurateur knew immediately who the money belonged to—the man who had sold him Lemongrass Pho & Thai Cuisine. But tracking him down proved challenging. The phone number on file no longer worked, leaving Yiengjuntuek searching for answers.

Determined to do right by the previous owner, Yiengjuntuek contacted local NBC affiliate WMBF to share his story, hoping the owner might see it on television. While live on air, something clicked: he remembered another phone number for the man, saved under a different name. He dialed it right there during the broadcast.

When the previous owner answered, Yiengjuntuek's relief was audible. "I have some good news for you," he told him. What made the moment even more meaningful was learning that the owner faced significant health struggles—making the returned money all the more impactful for the Myrtle Beach community.

Yiengjuntuek's message was clear: "Do the right thing. Don't give up honesty." His actions remind us that integrity still matters.
